req.title: Upload onboarding document — variant 3
req.statement: An employee shall upload onboarding documents up to 10MB in PDF, PNG, or JPG format; files outside this must be rejected. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines three orthogonal concern axes: (1) file size boundary at 10MB, (2) MIME type allow-list {PDF, PNG, JPG}, and (3) webhook emission on success. EP yields one positive partition (valid size+type) and two negative partitions (oversized, wrong type). BVA refines the size axis with an at-limit case (exactly 10MB). STC covers the state change from 'no document' to 'document stored + virus-scan queued'. DT captures the combined size×type decision. NEG explicitly exercises each error pathway (413, 415). The minimum required three tests are covered; additional tests address webhook emission and the combined decision table.

tests.name: test_upload_valid_pdf_under_limit_succeeds
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid session token
A PDF file of 1 MB exists on the test agent
actions: POST /documents/upload with multipart/form-data containing a 1 MB PDF file
Inspect HTTP response status and body
Verify virus-scan queue entry via <get_scan_queue_entry> helper
Verify webhook payload received by <mock_webhook_server> fixture
expected: Virus-scan queue contains the new document_id
Webhook event 'document.uploaded' is emitted with correct document_id
trace: R-WEB-079 positive path: valid PDF ≤ 10MB stored, scan queued, webhook fired
tests.executable:
def test_upload_valid_pdf_under_limit_succeeds(auth_session, mock_webhook_server):
    file_bytes = <generate_file_bytes>(size_mb=1, fmt="pdf")
    files = {"file": ("onboarding.pdf", file_bytes, "application/pdf")}
    resp = auth_session.post("/documents/upload", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    doc_id = body.get("document_id")
    assert doc_id, "document_id missing from response"
    queue_entry = <get_scan_queue_entry>(doc_id)
    assert queue_entry is not None, "virus-scan queue entry not found"
    event = mock_webhook_server.wait_for_event("document.uploaded", timeout=5)
    assert event["document_id"] == doc_id

tests.name: test_upload_file_exceeds_10mb_returns_413
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid session token
A PDF file of 11 MB exists on the test agent
actions: POST /documents/upload with multipart/form-data containing an 11 MB PDF file
Inspect HTTP response status and body
expected: No document stored in the system
tests.executable:
def test_upload_file_exceeds_10mb_returns_413(auth_session, mock_webhook_server):
    file_bytes = <generate_file_bytes>(size_mb=11, fmt="pdf")
    files = {"file": ("big.pdf", file_bytes, "application/pdf")}
    resp = auth_session.post("/documents/upload", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code == 413
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error") == "FILE_TOO_LARGE"
    mock_webhook_server.assert_no_event("document.uploaded", timeout=2)

tests.name: test_upload_exactly_10mb_pdf_is_accepted
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid session token
A PDF file of exactly 10485760 bytes (10 MiB) exists on the test agent
actions: POST /documents/upload with multipart/form-data containing a 10 MiB PDF file
Inspect HTTP response status and body
Verify virus-scan queue entry via <get_scan_queue_entry> helper
expected: HTTP 201 returned (boundary value is within the allowed range)
Virus-scan queue contains the new document_id
trace: R-WEB-079 boundary: file size == 10MB (upper inclusive boundary) must succeed
tests.executable:
def test_upload_exactly_10mb_pdf_is_accepted(auth_session):
    exact_bytes = <generate_file_bytes>(size_bytes=10 * 1024 * 1024, fmt="pdf")
    files = {"file": ("boundary.pdf", exact_bytes, "application/pdf")}
    resp = auth_session.post("/documents/upload", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    doc_id = resp.json().get("document_id")
    assert doc_id
    queue_entry = <get_scan_queue_entry>(doc_id)
    assert queue_entry is not None

tests.name: test_upload_disallowed_mime_type_returns_415
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid session token
A valid .exe file of 1 MB exists on the test agent
actions: POST /documents/upload with multipart/form-data containing a 1 MB .exe file with MIME application/octet-stream
Inspect HTTP response status and body
expected: No document stored in the system
trace: R-WEB-079 error pathway: MIME not in {PDF, PNG, JPG} → 415
tests.executable:
def test_upload_disallowed_mime_type_returns_415(auth_session, mock_webhook_server):
    file_bytes = <generate_file_bytes>(size_mb=1, fmt="exe")
    files = {"file": ("malware.exe", file_bytes, "application/octet-stream")}
    resp = auth_session.post("/documents/upload", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code == 415
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error") == "UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE"
    mock_webhook_server.assert_no_event("document.uploaded", timeout=2)

tests.name: test_upload_valid_png_and_jpg_both_accepted
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid session token
1 MB PNG and 1 MB JPG files exist on the test agent
actions: POST /documents/upload with a 1 MB PNG file (image/png)
POST /documents/upload with a 1 MB JPG file (image/jpeg)
expected: HTTP 201 for PNG upload; document_id present in body
HTTP 201 for JPG upload; document_id present in body
Both document_ids found in virus-scan queue
trace: R-WEB-079 decision table: rows {PNG, valid size} and {JPG, valid size} both map to accepted
tests.executable:
import pytest

@pytest.mark.parametrize("fmt,mime", [
    ("png", "image/png"),
    ("jpg", "image/jpeg"),
])
def test_upload_valid_png_and_jpg_both_accepted(auth_session, fmt, mime):
    file_bytes = <generate_file_bytes>(size_mb=1, fmt=fmt)
    files = {"file": (f"doc.{fmt}", file_bytes, mime)}
    resp = auth_session.post("/documents/upload", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    doc_id = resp.json().get("document_id")
    assert doc_id
    queue_entry = <get_scan_queue_entry>(doc_id)
    assert queue_entry is not None

tests.name: test_upload_transitions_document_state_to_stored_and_scan_queued
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid session token
No prior document with same name exists for this user (idempotency ensured by UUID filename)
actions: Confirm document does not exist before upload via <get_document_by_name> helper
POST /documents/upload with a valid 2 MB PDF file
Query virus-scan queue status via <get_scan_queue_entry> helper
trace: R-WEB-079 state transition: absent → stored; scan-queue: none → queued
tests.executable:
import uuid

def test_upload_transitions_document_state_to_stored_and_scan_queued(auth_session):
    unique_name = f"onboarding_{uuid.uuid4().hex}.pdf"
    pre = <get_document_by_name>(auth_session, unique_name)
    assert pre is None, "document should not exist before upload"
    file_bytes = <generate_file_bytes>(size_mb=2, fmt="pdf")
    files = {"file": (unique_name, file_bytes, "application/pdf")}
    resp = auth_session.post("/documents/upload", files=files)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    doc_id = resp.json()["document_id"]
    doc_status = <get_document_status>(doc_id)
    assert doc_status == "stored"
    scan_entry = <get_scan_queue_entry>(doc_id)
    assert scan_entry["status"] == "queued"
